title: UDS Component Figma Notes
description: Per-component open Figma findings surfaced by the figma-component-inspector — design questions, drift, explorations, and orphans that need designer attention. Lives at uds/components/<id>/figmanotes.json. Auto-pruned by the inspector on subsequent runs when the underlying condition is resolved (except kind=question, which is manual-only).

notes.description: List of open Figma notes for this component. Empty list (or missing file) means no open notes — the Figma Notes tab will not render.

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier for this note. Used by auto-prune to match the same note across inspector runs (so a note doesn't get added twice). Examples: 'logo-pill-exploration', 'outline-true-variant', 'page-orphans'.

kind.description: Category for the inspector's auto-prune logic. new-in-figma = new Figma node that isn't wired into the canonical component yet. figma-orphan = node sits in Figma but no longer attached to the canonical tree. drift = Figma value and code value disagree on something measurable. question = open question that doesn't fit the others; auto-prune does not apply.

title.description: One-line headline in plain language. No jargon ('canonical contract', 'unattested-by-figma', 'variant matrix'). Aim for what a designer would write to a teammate.

summary.description: 1-3 plain sentences explaining what was observed and where. May reference layer names, but keep them inline rather than as separate fields.

decisionNeeded.description: Optional question to the designer / owner. Phrased as a question, e.g. 'Should the new logo replace the simple one, or sit alongside as a variant?' Omit if the note is purely informational.

figmaRefs.description: Figma nodes referenced by this note. Used to deep-link from the UI and (for kinds that auto-prune) to check resolution on subsequent inspector runs.

name.description: Human-readable layer / component name as it appears in Figma.

nodeId.description: Figma node id, e.g. '6399:13744'. Used to construct the deep-link URL.

autoPruneWhen.description: Optional plain-language description of the condition that should auto-resolve this note. Examples: 'logo-pill becomes a nested instance in udc-nav-header', 'orphan dropdown is deleted from the nav-header Figma page'. Informational — the actual prune logic is in the inspector by kind.

raisedBy.description: Which agent or human raised this note. Usually 'figma-component-inspector'. Defaults to 'figma-component-inspector' if omitted.

raisedOn.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) the note was first surfaced.
